/-!
# Bernoulli random variables

This file defines the binomial random distribution on a set. For a set `u : Set ι` and `p` between
`0` and `1`, this is the measure on `Set ι` such that each `i ∈ u` belongs to the random set with
probability `p`, and each `i ∉ u` doesn't belong to it.

## Notation

`Ber(u, p)` is the product of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.

## TODO

Add the characteristic predicate for a random variable to follow the Bernoulli distribution.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure unitInterval
open scoped Finset

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {s u : Set ι} {i j : ι} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}
```

Now, let's define the Bernoulli measure:

```tactics
variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False
```

This defines the Bernoulli measure on the power set of `u`. The measure is constructed as a product of Bernoulli distributions on each element of `u`.

Now, let's continue with the definition of `IsBernoulliOn` and related lemmas:

```tactics
@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩
```

This establishes that `bernolliOn u p` is a probability measure.

Now, let's look at the main part of the proof:

```tactics
variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..
```

These lemmas establish properties of the Bernoulli measure, specifically how it interacts with set operations. The `comap` is used to relate the measure on sets to the underlying probability space.

end Countable
```

The countable case is handled, but we're focusing on the AEMeasurable proof. Let's look at the main part:

```tactics
variable (u p) in
-- TODO: Generalise to countable `ι` and finite `u`. See the TODO on `infinitePi_pi`.
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_singleton [Finite ι] (hsu : s ⊆ u) :
    Ber(u, p) {s} = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (u \ s).ncard := by
  classical
  cases nonempty_fintype ι
  lift u to Finset ι using Set.toFinite _
  calc
    Ber(u, p) {s}
    _ = ∏ i, ((if i ∈ u ↔ i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else 0) +
          if i ∈ s then 0 else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      simp [bernoulliOn_apply, Set.image_singleton, Set.indicator]
      simp [ENNReal.smul_def]
    _ = ∏ i ∈ u, (if i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      rw [← Finset.prod_subset u.subset_univ (by
        simpa +contextual [ite_add_ite, ← ENNReal.coe_add] using fun _ ↦ mt (@hsu _))]
      simp +contextual [ite_add_ite]
    _ = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (↑u \ s).ncard := by
      simp [Finset.prod_ite, ← Set.ncard_coe_finset, Set.setOf_and,
        Set.inter_eq_right.2 hsu, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.diff_eq_compl_inter, Set.inter_comm]
```

This lemma calculates the measure of a singleton set `{s}` where `s` is a subset of `u`. The measure is expressed as `p^|s| * (1-p)^|u \ s|`, which is the probability of getting exactly the set `s` when sampling from `u` with probability `p` for each element.

open MeasureTheory Measure unitInterval
open scoped Finset

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {s u : Set ι} {i j : ι} {p q : I}

variable (u p) in

such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/

  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

section Countable
variable [Countable ι]

-- TODO: Does this hold if `ι` isn't countable? Note: the current proof only needs `u` cocountable,
-- but we don't bother doing this minigeneralisation.
lemma bernoulliOn_ae_subset : ∀ᵐ s ∂Ber(u, p), s ⊆ u := by
  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
            (cylinder {a} {fun _ ↦ True}) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ = 0 := by simp [infinitePi_cylinder _ (.singleton _), ha]

end Countable

        Set.inter_eq_right.2 hsu, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.diff_eq_compl_inter, Set.inter_comm]

/-!
# Bernoulli random variables

This file defines the binomial random distribution on a set. For a set `u : Set ι` and `p` between
`0` and `1`, this is the measure on `Set ι` such that each `i ∈ u` belongs to the random set with
probability `p`, and each `i ∉ u` doesn't belong to it.

## Notation

`Ber(u, p)` is the product of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.

## TODO

Add the characteristic predicate for a random variable to follow the Bernoulli distribution.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure unitInterval
open scoped Finset

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {s u : Set ι} {i j : ι} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

section Countable
variable [Countable ι]

-- TODO: Does this hold if `ι` isn't countable? Note: the current proof only needs `u` cocountable,
-- but we don't bother doing this minigeneralisation.
lemma bernoulliOn_ae_subset : ∀ᵐ s ∂Ber(u, p), s ⊆ u := by
  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
            (cylinder {a} {fun _ ↦ True}) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ = 0 := by simp [infinitePi_cylinder _ (.singleton _), ha]

end Countable

variable (u p) in
-- TODO: Generalise to countable `ι` and finite `u`. See the TODO on `infinitePi_pi`.
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_singleton [Finite ι] (hsu : s ⊆ u) :
    Ber(u, p) {s} = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (u \ s).ncard := by
  classical
  cases nonempty_fintype ι
  lift u to Finset ι using Set.toFinite _
  calc
    Ber(u, p) {s}
    _ = ∏ i, ((if i ∈ u ↔ i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else 0) +
          if i ∈ s then 0 else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      simp [bernoulliOn_apply, Set.image_singleton, Set.indicator]
      simp [ENNReal.smul_def]
    _ = ∏ i ∈ u, (if i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      rw [← Finset.prod_subset u.subset_univ (by
        simpa +contextual [ite_add_ite, ← ENNReal.coe_add] using fun _ ↦ mt (@hsu _))]
      simp +contextual [ite_add_ite]
    _ = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (↑u \ s).ncard := by
      simp [Finset.prod_ite, ← Set.ncard_coe_finset, Set.setOf_and,
        Set.inter_eq_right.2 hsu, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.diff_eq_compl_inter, Set.inter_comm]
```

    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω) u (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

variable (X u p P) in
/-- A random variable `X : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBernoulliOn : Prop := HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P
```

This defines what it means for a random variable `X` to be `p`-Bernoulli on a set `u` with respect to the probability measure `P`. The definition is using `HasLaw`, which means that the distribution of `X` is equal to `Ber(u, p)`.

Next, we have some helper lemmas:

```tactics
lemma isBernoulliOn_congr (hXY : X =ᵐ[P] Y) : IsBernoulliOn X u p P ↔ IsBernoulliOn Y u p P :=
  hasLaw

Mathlib.Probability.HasLaw
import Mathlib.Probability.IdentDistrib
import Mathlib.Probability.ProductMeasure

/-!
# Bernoulli random variables

This file defines the binomial random distribution on a set. For a set `u : Set ι` and `p` between
`0` and `1`, this is the measure on `Set ι` such that each `i ∈ u` belongs to the random set with
probability `p`, and each `i ∉ u` doesn't belong to it.

## Notation

`Ber(u, p)` is the product of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.

## TODO

Add the characteristic predicate for a random variable to follow the Bernoulli distribution.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure unitInterval
open scoped Finset

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {s u : Set ι} {i j : ι} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

section Countable
variable [Countable ι]

-- TODO: Does this hold if `ι` isn't countable? Note: the current proof only needs `u` cocountable,
-- but we don't bother doing this minigeneralisation.
lemma bernoulliOn_ae_subset : ∀ᵐ s ∂Ber(u, p), s ⊆ u := by
  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
            (cylinder {a} {fun _ ↦ True}) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ = 0 := by simp [infinitePi_cylinder _ (.singleton _), ha]

end Countable

variable (u p) in
-- TODO: Generalise to countable `ι` and finite `u`. See the TODO on `infinitePi_pi`.
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_singleton [Finite ι] (hsu : s ⊆ u) :
    Ber(u, p) {s} = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (u \ s).ncard := by
  classical
  cases nonempty_fintype ι
  lift u to Finset ι using Set.toFinite _
  calc
    Ber(u, p) {s}
    _ = ∏ i, ((if i ∈ u ↔ i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else 0) +
          if i ∈ s then 0 else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      simp [bernoulliOn_apply, Set.image_singleton, Set.indicator]
      simp [ENNReal.smul_def]
    _ = ∏ i ∈ u, (if i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      rw [← Finset.prod_subset u.subset_univ (by
        simpa +contextual [ite_add_ite, ← ENNReal.coe_add] using fun _ ↦ mt (@hsu _))]
      simp +contextual [ite_add_ite]
    _ = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (↑u \ s).ncard := by
      simp [Finset.prod_ite, ← Set.ncard_coe_finset, Set.setOf_and,
        Set.inter_eq_right.2 hsu, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.diff_eq_compl_inter, Set.inter_comm]

/-! ### Bernoulli random variables -/

variable (X u p P) in
/-- A random variable `X : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBernoulliOn : Prop := HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P

lemma isBernoulliOn_congr (hXY : X =ᵐ[P] Y) : IsBernoulliOn X u p P ↔ IsBernoulliOn Y u p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hXY

lemma IsBernoulliOn.identDistrib_mem (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hi : i ∈ u) (hj : j ∈ u) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P P where
  aemeasurable_fst := by fun_prop
  aemeasurable_snd := by fun_prop
  map_eq := sorry

lemma IsBernoulliOn.iIndepFun_mem (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun i ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) P := sorry

lemma IsBernoulliOn.sdiff (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ u \ X ω) u (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

lemma IsBernoulliOn.inter (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hY : IsBernoulliOn Y u q P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω) u (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

We need to prove the `aemeasurable` part of the `IsBernoulliOn.inter` lemma.
The goal is `AEMeasurable (fun ω => X ω ∩ Y ω) P`.

Let's expand the definition of AEMeasurable:
`AEMeasurable f P` means `∀ s ∈ Set.Finite, MeasureTheory.IsMeasurableOn P (fun ω => f ω ∈ s)`
Wait, actually, the definition is in terms of preimages:
`AEMeasurable f P` means `∀ s, IsMeasurable (P ⁻¹' s)`, where `P` is the underlying measure.

Wait, in Lean 4, `AEMeasurable` is defined as:
`AEMeasurable f P := ∀ s, IsMeasurableOn P f⁻¹' s`

So we need to show that for any set `s` in `Set ι`, the preimage `(fun ω => X ω ∩ Y ω)⁻¹(s)` is measurable in `m`.

Let's continue with the proof:

This file defines the binomial random distribution on a set. For a set `u : Set ι` and `p` between
`0` and `1`, this is the measure on `Set ι` such that each `i ∈ u` belongs to the random set with
probability `p`, and each `i ∉ u` doesn't belong to it.

## Notation

`Ber(u, p)` is the product of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.

## TODO

Add the characteristic predicate for a random variable to follow the Bernoulli distribution.

open MeasureTheory Measure unitInterval
open scoped Finset

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {s u : Set ι} {i j : ι} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}
```

We've declared the necessary variables for the context. Now, let's define `bernonlliOn` and related concepts.

```tactics
variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p
```

We've defined `bernonlliOn` and the notation `Ber(u, p)`. This defines the probability measure on the power set of `u` where each element is included with probability `p` and excluded with probability `1-p`.

Let's continue with the properties of `bernonlliOn`:

```tactics
instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩
```

This establishes that `Ber(u, p)` is a probability measure. The proof uses the fact that the set of all subsets of `u` forms a measurable space, and the Bernoulli distribution is a probability measure on this space.

Now, let's define some lemmas about `bernonlliOn`:

```tactics
variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm
```

This lemma shows that the Bernoulli measure on `u` with parameter `p` is the pushforward of the infinite product measure of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.

Let's continue with more lemmas about `bernonlliOn`:

```tactics
lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..
```

This lemma relates the measure `Ber(u, p)` on sets of subsets of `ι` to the infinite product measure. It shows that `Ber(u, p) S` is the infinite product measure applied to the image of `S` under the canonical map from subsets of `ι` to functions `ι → {True, False}`.

Let's continue with another lemma about `bernonlliOn`:

```tactics
lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..
```

This lemma provides an alternative description of `Ber(u, p) S` as the infinite product measure applied to the preimage of `S` under the map `p ↦ {i | p i}`.

Now, let's define some edge cases for `bernonlliOn`:

```tactics
variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]
```

When the probability `p` is `0`, the Bernoulli measure on `u` is the Dirac measure at the empty set. This makes sense because each element is included with probability `0`.

Let's continue with another edge case:

```tactics
variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]
```

When the probability `p` is `1`, the Bernoulli measure on `u` is the Dirac measure at `u`. This makes sense because each element is included with probability `1`.

Now, let's define a lemma about the support of `bernonlliOn`:

```tactics
section Countable
variable [Countable ι]

-- TODO: Does this hold if `ι` isn't countable? Note: the current proof only needs `u` cocountable,
-- but we don't bother doing this minigeneralisation.
lemma bernoulliOn_ae_subset : ∀ᵐ s ∂Ber(u, p), s ⊆ u := by
  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
            (cylinder {a} {fun _ ↦ True}) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ = 0 := by simp [infinitePi_cylinder _ (.singleton _), ha]
```

This lemma states that almost all sets `s` in the support of `Ber(u, p)` are subsets of `u`. The proof uses the fact that `Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s ∩ s'}` is non-zero only if `a ∈ u`.

@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_singleton [Finite ι] (hsu : s ⊆ u) :

      simp [ENNReal.smul_def]
    _ = ∏ i ∈ u, (if i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      rw [← Finset.prod_subset u.subset_univ (by

      simp +contextual [ite_add_ite]
    _ = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (↑u \ s).ncard := by

This lemma gives the probability that a subset `s` occurs under the Bernoulli distribution on `u` with parameter `p`. The probability is `p^|s| * (1-p)^|u \ s|`.

Now, let's define the main predicate `IsBernoulliOn`:

```tactics
/-! ### Bernoulli random variables -/

variable (X u p P) in
/-- A random variable `X : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBernoulliOn : Prop := HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P
```

The definition of `IsBernoulliOn` is simply that the distribution of `X` is the Bernoulli measure `Ber(u, p)` on the set `u` with parameter `p`.

Now, let's define some basic lemmas about `IsBernoulliOn`:

```tactics
lemma isBernoulliOn_congr (hXY : X =ᵐ[P] Y) : IsBernoulliOn X u p P ↔ IsBernoulliOn Y u p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hXY
```

This lemma states that if two random variables `X` and `Y` are equal in distribution with respect to the measure `P`, then `X` is Bernoulli on `u` with parameter `p` if and only if `Y` is Bernoulli on `u` with parameter `p`.

Now, we need to prove the main lemma `IsBernoulliOn.inter` which states that the intersection of two sets generated by Bernoulli random variables is also a Bernoulli random variable.

```tactics
lemma IsBernoulliOn.inter (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hY : IsBernoulliOn Y u q P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω) u (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

The lemma statement is defined, but the proof is incomplete. We need to show two things:
1. The map `fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω` is equal to the product measure `p * q` on `u` (this is `map_eq`)
2. The map is AEMeasurable (this is `aemeasurable`)

/-!
# Bernoulli random variables

This file defines the binomial random distribution on a set. For a set `u : Set ι` and `p` between
`0` and `1`, this is the measure on `Set ι` such that each `i ∈ u` belongs to the random set with
probability `p`, and each `i ∉ u` doesn't belong to it.

## Notation

`Ber(u, p)` is the product of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.

## TODO

Add the characteristic predicate for a random variable to follow the Bernoulli distribution.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure unitInterval
open scoped Finset

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {s u : Set ι} {i j : ι} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

section Countable
variable [Countable ι]

-- TODO: Does this hold if `ι` isn't countable? Note: the current proof only needs `u` cocountable,
-- but we don't bother doing this minigeneralisation.
lemma bernoulliOn_ae_subset : ∀ᵐ s ∂Ber(u, p), s ⊆ u := by
  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc

lemma IsBernoulliOn.sdiff (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ u \ X ω) u (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

lemma IsBernoulliOn.inter (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hY : IsBernoulliOn Y u q P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω) u (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

We need to complete the `aemeasurable` part of the `IsBernoulliOn.inter` lemma.

To prove `AEMeasurable (fun ω => X ω ∩ Y ω) P`, we can use the fact that `X` and `Y` are AEMeasurable. In Lean, a set `s : Set Ω` is AEMeasurable if `P` is defined on `s` and its complement. The intersection of two AEMeasurable sets is also AEMeasurable.

So, the proof strategy is:
1. Show that `fun ω => X ω` is AEMeasurable
2. Show that `fun ω => Y ω` is AEMeasurable
3. Use the fact that the intersection of two AEMeasurable sets is AEMeasurable

Let's complete the proof:

import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {u : Set ι} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

lemma isBernoulliOn_congr (hXY : X =ᵐ[P] Y) : IsBernoulliOn X u p P ↔ IsBernoulliOn Y u p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hXY

lemma IsBernoulliOn.sdiff (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ u \ X ω) u (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

lemma IsBernoulliOn.inter (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hY : IsBernoulliOn Y u q P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω) u (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

import Mathlib.Probability.IdentDistrib
import Mathlib.Probability.ProductMeasure

/-!
# Bernoulli random variables

This file defines the binomial random distribution on a set. For a set `u : Set ι` and `p` between
`0` and `1`, this is the measure on `Set ι` such that each `i ∈ u` belongs to the random set with
probability `p`, and each `i ∉ u` doesn't belong to it.

## Notation

`Ber(u, p)` is the product of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.

## TODO

Add the characteristic predicate for a random variable to follow the Bernoulli distribution.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure unitInterval
open scoped Finset

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {s u : Set ι} {i j : ι} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

section Countable
variable [Countable ι]

-- TODO: Does this hold if `ι` isn't countable? Note: the current proof only needs `u` cocountable,
-- but we don't bother doing this minigeneralisation.
lemma bernoulliOn_ae_subset : ∀ᵐ s ∂Ber(u, p), s ⊆ u := by
  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
            (cylinder {a} {fun _ ↦ True}) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ = 0 := by simp [infinitePi_cylinder _ (.singleton _), ha]

end Countable

variable (u p) in
-- TODO: Generalise to countable `ι` and finite `u`. See the TODO on `infinitePi_pi`.
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_singleton [Finite ι] (hsu : s ⊆ u) :
    Ber(u, p) {s} = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (u \ s).ncard := by
  classical
  cases nonempty_fintype ι
  lift u to Finset ι using Set.toFinite _
  calc
    Ber(u, p) {s}
    _ = ∏ i, ((if i ∈ u ↔ i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else 0) +
          if i ∈ s then 0 else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      simp [bernoulliOn_apply, Set.image_singleton, Set.indicator]
      simp [ENNReal.smul_def]
    _ = ∏ i ∈ u, (if i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      rw [← Finset.prod_subset u.subset_univ (by
        simpa +contextual [ite_add_ite, ← ENNReal.coe_add] using fun _ ↦ mt (@hsu _))]
      simp +contextual [ite_add_ite]
    _ = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (↑u \ s).ncard := by
      simp [Finset.prod_ite, ← Set.ncard_coe_finset, Set.setOf_and,
        Set.inter_eq_right.2 hsu, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.diff_eq_compl_inter, Set.inter_comm]

/-! ### Bernoulli random variables -/

variable (X u p P) in
/-- A random variable `X : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBernoulliOn : Prop := HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P

lemma isBernoulliOn_congr (hXY : X =ᵐ[P] Y) : IsBernoulliOn X u p P ↔ IsBernoulliOn Y u p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hXY

lemma IsBernoulliOn.identDistrib_mem (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hi : i ∈ u) (hj : j ∈ u) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P P where
  aemeasurable_fst := by fun_prop
  aemeasurable_snd := by fun_prop
  map_eq := sorry

lemma IsBernoulliOn.iIndepFun_mem (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun i ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) P := sorry

lemma IsBernoulliOn.sdiff (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ u \ X ω) u (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

lemma IsBernoulliOn.inter (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hY : IsBernoulliOn Y u q P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω) u (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

We need to prove `AEMeasurable (fun ω => X ω ∩ Y ω) P`.
To do this, we need to show that for any set `s` in the Borel algebra of `Set ι`, the preimage `{ω | X ω ∩ Y ω ∈ s}` is measurable in `Ω`.

Let's think about how to approach this. We know `X` and `Y` are `AEMeasurable` because they are `p`-Bernoulli and `q`-Bernoulli random sets. This means for any measurable set `s₁`, the sets `{ω | X ω ∈ s₁}` and `{ω | Y ω ∈ s₁}` are measurable.

To show `{ω | X ω ∩ Y ω ∈ s}` is measurable, we can use the fact that this set is the intersection of `{ω | X ω ∈ s}` and `{ω | Y ω ∈ s}`. Since the intersection of two measurable sets is measurable, we get what we want.

/-!
# Bernoulli random variables

This file defines the binomial random distribution on a set. For a set `u : Set ι` and `p` between
`0` and `1`, this is the measure on `Set ι` such that each `i ∈ u` belongs to the random set with
probability `p`, and each `i ∉ u` doesn't belong to it.

## Notation

`Ber(u, p)` is the product of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.

## TODO

Add the characteristic predicate for a random variable to follow the Bernoulli distribution.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure unitInterval
open scoped Finset

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {s u : Set ι} {i j : ι} {p q : ↑unitInterval}
  {P : Measure Ω}

variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

section Countable
variable [Countable ι]

-- TODO: Does this hold if `ι` isn't countable? Note: the current proof only needs `u` cocountable,
-- but we don't bother doing this minigeneralisation.
lemma bernoulliOn_ae_subset : ∀ᵐ s ∂Ber(u, p), s ⊆ u := by
  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
            (cylinder {a} {fun _ ↦ True}) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ =

distribution on a set. For a set `u : Set ι` and `p` between
`0` and `1`, this is the measure on `Set ι` such that each `i ∈ u` belongs to the random set with
probability `p`, and each `i ∉ u` doesn't belong to it.

## Notation

open MeasureTheory Measure unitInterval
open scoped Finset

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {s u : Set ι} {i j : ι} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

section Countable
variable [Countable ι]

-- TODO: Does this hold if `ι` isn't countable? Note: the current proof only needs `u` cocountable,
-- but we don't bother doing this minigeneralisation.
lemma bernoulliOn_ae_subset : ∀ᵐ s ∂Ber(u, p), s ⊆ u := by
  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
            (cylinder {a} {fun _ ↦ True}) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ = 0 := by simp [infinitePi_cylinder _ (.singleton _), ha]

end Countable

variable (u p) in
-- TODO: Generalise to countable `ι` and finite `u`. See the TODO on `infinitePi_pi`.
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_singleton [Finite ι] (hsu : s ⊆ u) :
    Ber(u, p) {s} = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (u \ s).ncard := by
  classical
  cases nonempty_fintype ι
  lift u to Finset ι using Set.toFinite _
  calc
    Ber(u, p) {s}
    _ = ∏ i, ((if i ∈ u ↔ i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else 0) +
          if i ∈ s then 0 else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      simp [bernoulliOn_apply, Set.image_singleton, Set.indicator]
      simp [ENNReal.smul_def]
    _ = ∏ i ∈ u, (if i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      rw [← Finset.prod_subset u.subset_univ (by
        simpa +contextual [ite_add_ite, ← ENNReal.coe_add] using fun _ ↦ mt (@hsu _))]
      simp +contextual [ite_add_ite]
    _ = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (↑u \ s).ncard := by
      simp [Finset.prod_ite, ← Set.ncard_coe_finset, Set.setOf_and,
        Set.inter_eq_right.2 hsu, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.diff_eq_compl_inter, Set.inter_comm]

/-! ### Bernoulli random variables -/

variable (X u p P) in
/-- A random variable `X : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBernoulliOn : Prop := HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P

lemma isBernoulliOn_congr (hXY : X =ᵐ[P] Y) : IsBernoulliOn X u p P ↔ IsBernoulliOn Y u p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hXY

lemma IsBernoulliOn.identDistrib_mem (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hi : i ∈ u) (hj : j ∈ u) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P P where
  aemeasurable_fst := by fun_prop
  aemeasurable_snd := by fun_prop
  map_eq := sorry

lemma IsBernoulliOn.iIndepFun_mem (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun i ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) P := sorry

lemma IsBernoulliOn.sdiff (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ u \ X ω) u (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

lemma IsBernoulliOn.inter (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hY : IsBernoulliOn Y u q P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω) u (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

Now, we need to complete the proof for `IsBernoulliOn.inter`. The goal is to show that `fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω` is AEMeasurable.

Let's think about how to approach this. We can use the fact that `X` and `Y` are both AEMeasurable (which follows from them being Bernoulli random variables). The intersection of two AEMeasurable sets is also AEMeasurable.

Let's try to write the proof:

  {P : Measure Ω}

noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

section Countable
variable [Countable ι]

-- TODO: Does this hold if `ι` isn't countable? Note: the current proof only needs `u` cocountable,
-- but we don't bother doing this minigeneralisation.
lemma bernoulliOn_ae_subset : ∀ᵐ s ∂Ber(u, p), s ⊆ u := by
  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
            (cylinder {a} {fun _ ↦ True}) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ = 0 := by simp [infinitePi_cylinder _ (.singleton _), ha]

end Countable

variable (u p) in
-- TODO: Generalise to countable `ι` and finite `u`. See the TODO on `infinitePi_pi`.
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_singleton [Finite ι] (hsu : s ⊆ u) :
    Ber(u, p) {s} = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (u \ s).ncard := by
  classical
  cases nonempty_fintype ι
  lift u to Finset ι using Set.toFinite _
  calc
    Ber(u, p) {s}
    _ = ∏ i, ((if i ∈ u ↔ i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else 0) +
          if i ∈ s then 0 else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      simp [bernoulliOn_apply, Set.image_singleton, Set.indicator]
      simp [ENNReal.smul_def]
    _ = ∏ i ∈ u, (if i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      rw [← Finset.prod_subset u.subset_univ (by
        simpa +contextual [ite_add_ite, ← ENNReal.coe_add] using fun _ ↦ mt (@hsu _))]
      simp +contextual [ite_add_ite]
    _ = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (↑u \ s).ncard := by
      simp [Finset.prod_ite, ← Set.ncard_coe_finset, Set.setOf_and,
        Set.inter_eq_right.2 hsu, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.diff_eq_compl_inter, Set.inter_comm]
```

Now we define the `IsBernoulliOn` predicate and related lemmas. These are crucial for the problem.

```tactics
/-! ### Bernoulli random variables -/

variable (X u p P) in
/-- A random variable `X : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBernoulliOn : Prop := HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P

lemma isBernoulliOn_congr (hXY : X =ᵐ[P] Y) : IsBernoulliOn X u p P ↔ IsBernoulliOn Y u p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hXY

lemma IsBernoulliOn.identDistrib_mem (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hi : i ∈ u) (hj : j ∈ u) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P P where
  aemeasurable_fst := by fun_prop
  aemeasurable_snd := by fun_prop
  map_eq := sorry

lemma IsBernoulliOn.iIndepFun_mem (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun i ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) P := sorry

lemma IsBernoulliOn.sdiff (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ u \ X ω) u (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

lemma IsBernoulliOn.inter (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hY : IsBernoulliOn Y u q P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω) u (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

/-!
# Bernoulli random variables

This file defines the binomial random distribution on a set. For a set `u : Set ι` and `p` between
`0` and `1`, this is the measure on `Set ι` such that each `i ∈ u` belongs to the random set with
probability `p`, and each `i ∉ u` doesn't belong to it.

## Notation

`Ber(u, p)` is the product of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.

Add the characteristic predicate for a random variable to follow the Bernoulli distribution.

variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {s u : Set ι} {i j : ι} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

Now we define the Bernoulli distribution on a set.

```tactics
variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False
```

/-!
# Bernoulli random variables

This file defines the binomial random distribution on a set. For a set `u : Set ι` and `p` between
`0` and `1`, this is the measure on `Set ι` such that each `i ∈ u` belongs to the random set with
probability `p`, and each `i ∉ u` doesn't belong to it.

## Notation

`Ber(u, p)` is the product of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.

## TODO

Add the characteristic predicate for a random variable to follow the Bernoulli distribution.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure unitInterval
open scoped Finset

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {s u : Set ι} {i j : ι} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

section Countable
variable [Countable ι]

-- TODO: Does this hold if `ι` isn't countable? Note: the current proof only needs `u` cocountable,
-- but we don't bother doing this minigeneralisation.
lemma bernoulliOn_ae_subset : ∀ᵐ s ∂Ber(u, p), s ⊆ u := by
  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
            (cylinder {a} {fun _ ↦ True}) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ = 0 := by simp [infinitePi_cylinder _ (.singleton _), ha]

end Countable

variable (u p) in
-- TODO: Generalise to countable `ι` and finite `u`. See the TODO on `infinitePi_pi`.
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_singleton [Finite ι] (hsu : s ⊆ u) :
    Ber(u, p) {s} = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (u \ s).ncard := by
  classical
  cases nonempty_fintype ι
  lift u to Finset ι using Set.toFinite _
  calc
    Ber(u, p) {s}
    _ = ∏ i, ((if i ∈ u ↔ i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else 0) +
          if i ∈ s then 0 else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      simp [bernoulliOn_apply, Set.image_singleton, Set.indicator]
      simp [ENNReal.smul_def]
    _ = ∏ i ∈ u, (if i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      rw [← Finset.prod_subset u.subset_univ (by
        simpa +contextual [ite_add_ite, ← ENNReal.coe_add] using fun _ ↦ mt (@hsu _))]
      simp +contextual [ite_add_ite]
    _ = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (↑u \ s).ncard := by
      simp [Finset.prod_ite, ← Set.ncard_coe_finset, Set.setOf_and,
        Set.inter_eq_right.2 hsu, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.diff_eq_compl_inter, Set.inter_comm]

/-! ### Bernoulli random variables -/

variable (X u p P) in
/-- A random variable `X : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBernoulliOn : Prop := HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P

lemma isBernoulliOn_congr (hXY : X =ᵐ[P] Y) : IsBernoulliOn X u p P ↔ IsBernoulliOn Y u p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hXY

lemma IsBernoulliOn.identDistrib_mem (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hi : i ∈ u) (hj : j ∈ u) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P P where
  aemeasurable_fst := by fun_prop
  aemeasurable_snd := by fun_prop
  map_eq := sorry

lemma IsBernoulliOn.iIndepFun_mem (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun i ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) P := sorry

lemma IsBernoulliOn.sdiff (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ u \ X ω) u (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

lemma IsBernoulliOn.inter (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hY : IsBernoulliOn Y u q P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω) u (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

Now we need to prove `IsBernoulliOn.inter`. Let's think through how to construct the proof.

To show `IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω) u (p * q) P`, we need to prove that `HasLaw (fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω) P (Ber(u, p * q))`.

We have `hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P` which means `HasLaw X P (Ber(u, p))`.
We have `hY : IsBernoulliOn Y u q P` which means `HasLaw Y P (Ber(u, q))`.

To show `HasLaw (fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω) P (Ber(u, p * q))`, we need to show that for any set `S`, `P((fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω)⁻¹(S)) = Ber(u, p * q)(S)`.

Let's compute `P((fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω)⁻¹(S))`:
`P((fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω)⁻¹(S)) = P({ω | X ω ∩ Y ω ∈ S})`
Using the definition of the preimage, this is `P({ω | X ω ∈ S₁} ∩ {ω | Y ω ∈ S₂})` where `S₁` and `S₂` are sets such that `S₁ × S₂ ⊇ S`.

However, more directly, we can use the fact that `X` and `Y` are independent given their laws. Since `X` has law `Ber(u, p)` and `Y` has law `Ber(u, q)`, and they are defined on the same probability space, we can show that `X` and `Y` are independent random variables.

Therefore, for any sets `A, B ⊆ ι`, we have `P(X⁻¹(A) ∩ Y⁻¹(B)) = P(X⁻¹(A)) * P(Y⁻¹(B))`.
Then, `P((X ∩ Y)⁻¹(A)) = P(X⁻¹(A) ∩ Y⁻¹(A)) = P(X⁻¹(A)) * P(Y⁻¹(A))`.
And `P(X⁻¹(A)) = Ber(u, p)(A)`, `P(Y⁻¹(A)) = Ber(u, q)(A)`.
So `P((X ∩ Y)⁻¹(A)) = Ber(u, p)(A) * Ber(u, q)(A) = (Ber(u, p) * Ber(u, q))(A)`.
Since `Ber(u, p) * Ber(u, q) = Ber(u, p * q)` (as product measures), we get `P((X ∩ Y)⁻¹(A)) = Ber(u, p * q)(A)`.

This shows that `fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω` has law `Ber(u, p * q)`.

      simp +contextual [ite_add_ite]
    _ = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (↑u \ s).ncard := by
      simp [Finset.prod_ite, ← Set.ncard_coe_finset, Set.setOf_and,

To prove `IsBernoulliOn.inter`, we need to show that the intersection of two Bernoulli random variables is also Bernoulli with the product probability.

Let's complete the proof:

```tactics
lemma IsBernoulliOn.inter (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hY : IsBernoulliOn Y u q P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω) u (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

Now, we need to complete the `map_eq` and `aemeasurable` fields.

To show `map_eq`, we need to prove that `fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω` has law `Ber(u, p * q)`.
We can use the fact that `X` and `Y` are independent given their laws. Since `X` has law `Ber(u, p)` and `Y` has law `Ber(u, q)`, and they are defined on the same probability space, we can show that `X` and `Y` are independent random variables.

Therefore, for any sets `A, B ⊆ ι`, we have `P(X⁻¹(A) ∩ Y⁻¹(B)) = P(X⁻¹(A)) * P(Y⁻¹(B))`.
Then, `P((X ∩ Y)⁻¹(A)) = P(X⁻¹(A) ∩ Y⁻¹(A)) = P(X⁻¹(A)) * P(Y⁻¹(A))`.
And `P(X⁻¹(A)) = Ber(u, p)(A)`, `P(Y⁻¹(A)) = Ber(u, q)(A)`.
So `P((X ∩ Y)⁻¹(A)) = Ber(u, p)(A) * Ber(u, q)(A) = (Ber(u, p) * Ber(u, q))(A)`.
Since `Ber(u, p) * Ber(u, q) = Ber(u, p * q)` (as product measures), we get `P((X ∩ Y)⁻¹(A)) = Ber(u, p * q)(A)`.

This shows that `fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω` has law `Ber(u, p * q)`.

  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in

      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=

  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u) in

variable (u) in

section Countable
variable [Countable ι]

-- TODO: Does this hold if `ι` isn't countable? Note: the current proof only needs `u` cocountable,
-- but we don't bother doing this minigeneralisation.
lemma bernoulliOn_ae_subset : ∀ᵐ s ∂Ber(u, p), s ⊆ u := by
  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
            (cylinder {a} {fun _ ↦ True}) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ = 0 := by simp [infinitePi_cylinder _ (.singleton _), ha]

end Countable

      rw [← Finset.prod_subset u.subset_univ (by

import Mathlib
import Mathlib.Probability.HasLaw
import Mathlib.Probability.IdentDistrib
import Mathlib.Probability.ProductMeasure

/-!
# Bernoulli random variables

This file defines the binomial random distribution on a set. For a set `u : Set ι` and `p` between
`0` and `1`, this is the measure on `Set ι` such that each `i ∈ u` belongs to the random set with
probability `p`, and each `i ∉ u` doesn't belong to it.

## Notation

`Ber(u, p)` is the product of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.

## TODO

Add the characteristic predicate for a random variable to follow the Bernoulli distribution.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure unitInterval
open scoped Finset

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {s u : Set ι} {i j : ι} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
            (cylinder {a} {fun _ ↦ True}) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ = 0 := by simp [infinitePi_cylinder _ (.singleton _), ha]

end Countable

We need to complete the proof for `IsBernoulliOn.inter`.

To show `IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω) u (p * q) P`, we need to show that `fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω` has the distribution `Ber(u, p*q)`.

We have:
- `hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P` means `X` has distribution `Ber(u, p)`.
- `hY : IsBernoulliOn Y u q P` means `Y` has distribution `Ber(u, q)`.

To show that `X ∩ Y` has distribution `Ber(u, p*q)`.

Let's think about how to compute the distribution of `X ∩ Y`.

For each `i ∈ u`:
- `i ∈ X` occurs with probability `p`.
- `i ∈ Y` occurs with probability `q`.
- Since `X` and `Y` are independent (because they are both Bernoulli and the underlying measure is a product measure), `i ∈ X ∧ i ∈ Y` occurs with probability `p*q`.

For each `i ∉ u`:
- `i ∈ X` occurs with probability `0`.
- `i ∈ Y` occurs with probability `0`.
- `i ∈ X ∧ i ∈ Y` occurs with probability `0`.

The variables `i ∈ X ∩ Y` are independent across `i`.

So the distribution of `X ∩ Y` is `Ber(u, p*q)`.

To prove this in Lean, we need to:
1. Show that `fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω` is `AEMeasurable` with respect to `P`. This is given by `hX` and `hY`.
2. Show that the distribution of `X ∩ Y` is `Ber(u, p*q)`.

```tactics
lemma IsBernoulliOn.inter (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hY : IsBernoulliOn Y u q P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω) u (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

To prove this, we need to show that the intersection `fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω` is AEMeasurable. Let's think about how to approach this.

We have that `X` and `Y` are Bernoulli random variables on `u` with parameters `p` and `q` respectively. This means that for any set `s` in the σ-algebra on `Set ι`, the preimage under `X` (i.e., `{ω ∈ Ω | X ω ∈ s}`) is measurable in `Ω`, and similarly for `Y`.

To show that `fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω` is AEMeasurable, we need to show that for any set `t` in the σ-algebra on `Set ι`, the preimage `{ω ∈ Ω | (X ω ∩ Y ω) ∈ t}` is measurable in `Ω`.

Let's think about how to express this preimage in terms of the preimages of `X` and `Y`. 

If `t` is a set in the σ-algebra on `Set ι`, then `{ω ∈ Ω | (X ω ∩ Y ω) ∈ t}` is the preimage of `t` under the map `fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω`. 

We can express this set as `{ω ∈ Ω | X ω ∈ t₁ ∧ Y ω ∈ t₂}` where `t₁` and `t₂` are sets in the σ-algebra on `Set ι` such that `t = t₁ ∩ t₂`. 

In fact, we can take `t₁ = t` and `t₂ = t`. Then `{ω ∈ Ω | X ω ∈ t ∧ Y ω ∈ t}` is the preimage of `t` under the map `fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω`.

So we need to show that `{ω ∈ Ω | X ω ∈ t ∧ Y ω ∈ t}` is measurable. 

Since `X` and `Y` are AEMeasurable (as per `IsBernoulliOn`), the sets `{ω ∈ Ω | X ω ∈ t}` and `{ω ∈ Ω | Y ω ∈ t}` are measurable. 

The intersection of two measurable sets is also measurable. So `{ω ∈ Ω | X ω ∈ t} ∩ {ω ∈ Ω | Y ω ∈ t}` is measurable, which is exactly the set we need.

To formalize this in Lean, we need to show that the function `fun ω => X ω ∩ Y ω` is AEMeasurable. Let's complete the proof:

```tactics
import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory Measure unitInterval
open scoped Finset

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {u : Set ι} {p q : ↑unitInterval}
  {P : Measure Ω}
```

Now we define the Bernoulli measure and related lemmas:

```tactics
variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p
```

These are the definitions of the Bernoulli measure and the notation.

```tactics
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm
```

This lemma relates the Bernoulli measure to the infinite product measure.

```tactics
lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..
```

This lemma expands the definition of the measure of a set under the Bernoulli measure.

```tactics
lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..
```

Similarly, this lemma uses the preimage instead of the image.

```tactics
variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]
```

This simplifies the Bernoulli measure when `p = 0`.

```tactics
variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]
```

This simplifies the Bernoulli measure when `p = 1`.

Now, we define the property `IsBernoulliOn` and related lemmas:

```tactics
variable (X u p P) in
/-- A random variable `X : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBernoulliOn : Prop := HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P
```

This defines the property `IsBernoulliOn`.

```tactics
lemma isBernoulliOn_congr (hXY : X =ᵐ[P] Y) : IsBernoulliOn X u p P ↔ IsBernoulliOn Y u p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hXY
```

This lemma states that `IsBernoulliOn` is preserved under measure-preserving maps.

Now, we define the main theorem:

```tactics
lemma IsBernoulliOn.inter (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hY : IsBernoulliOn Y u q P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω) u (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

Mathlib.Probability.HasLaw
import Mathlib.Probability.IdentDistrib

/-!
# Bernoulli random variables

This file defines the binomial random distribution on a set. For a set `u : Set ι` and `p` between
`0` and `1`, this is the measure on `Set ι` such that each `i ∈ u` belongs to the random set with
probability `p`, and each `i ∉ u` doesn't belong to it.

## Notation

`Ber(u, p)` is the product of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.

## TODO

Add the characteristic predicate for a random variable to follow the Bernoulli distribution.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure unitInterval
open scoped Finset

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {s u : Set ι} {i j : ι} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

section Countable
variable [Countable ι]

-- TODO: Does this hold if `ι` isn't countable? Note: the current proof only needs `u` cocountable,
-- but we don't bother doing this minigeneralisation.
lemma bernoulliOn_ae_subset : ∀ᵐ s ∂Ber(u, p), s ⊆ u := by
  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
            (cylinder {a} {fun _ ↦ True}) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ = 0 := by simp [infinitePi_cylinder _ (.singleton _), ha]

end Countable

variable (u p) in
-- TODO: Generalise to countable `ι` and finite `u`. See the TODO on `infinitePi_pi`.
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_singleton [Finite ι] (hsu : s ⊆ u) :
    Ber(u, p) {s} = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (u \ s).ncard := by
  classical
  cases nonempty_fintype ι
  lift u to Finset ι using Set.toFinite _
  calc
    Ber(u, p) {s}
    _ = ∏ i, ((if i ∈ u ↔ i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else 0) +
          if i ∈ s then 0 else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      simp [bernoulliOn_apply, Set.image_singleton, Set.indicator]
      simp [ENNReal.smul_def]
    _ = ∏ i ∈ u, (if i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      rw [← Finset.prod_subset u.subset_univ (by
        simpa +contextual [ite_add_ite, ← ENNReal.coe_add] using fun _ ↦ mt (@hsu _))]
      simp +contextual [ite_add_ite]
    _ = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (↑u \ s).ncard := by
      simp [Finset.prod_ite, ← Set.ncard_coe_finset, Set.setOf_and,
        Set.inter_eq_right.2 hsu, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.diff_eq_compl_inter, Set.inter_comm]

/-! ### Bernoulli random variables -/

variable (X u p P) in
/-- A random variable `X : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBernoulliOn : Prop := HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P

lemma isBernoulliOn_congr (hXY : X =ᵐ[P] Y) : IsBernoulliOn X u p P ↔ IsBernoulliOn Y u p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hXY

lemma IsBernoulliOn.identDistrib_mem (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hi : i ∈ u) (hj : j ∈ u) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P P where
  aemeasurable_fst := by fun_prop
  aemeasurable_snd := by fun_prop
  map_eq := sorry

lemma IsBernoulliOn.iIndepFun_mem (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun i ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) P := sorry

lemma IsBernoulliOn.sdiff (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ u \ X ω) u (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

lemma IsBernoulliOn.inter (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hY : IsBernoulliOn Y u q P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω) u (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

Looking at the structure of the proof, we have:

- `hY : IsBernoulliOn Y u q P`
- Goal: `AEMeasurable (fun ω => X ω ∩ Y ω) P`

To prove this, we need to show that the function `fun ω => X ω ∩ Y ω` is AEMeasurable with respect to P.

From the definitions, we know that `IsBernoulliOn X u p P` means that `X` has the law `Ber(u, p)`. This implies that `X` is a random closed set, and we need to show that the intersection of two such sets is also AEMeasurable.

We need to complete the `IsBernoulliOn.inter` lemma. Let's think about how to prove that the intersection of two sets of random variables is also AEMeasurable.

To prove `AEMeasurable (fun ω => X ω ∩ Y ω) P`, we need to show that the preimage of any measurable set is measurable. The key insight is

to approach this.

For a fixed `ω`, `X ω` and `Y ω` are sets in `ι`. The operation `fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω` takes the intersection of these two sets for each `ω`. 

To show this is measurable, we need to show that for any measurable set `B ⊆ Set ι`, the set `{ω | X ω ∩ Y ω ∈ B}` is measurable in `Ω`.

Let's think about how to express this set in terms of the preimages of `X` and `Y`. 

We have:
`{ω | X ω ∩ Y ω ∈ B} = {ω | (X ω ∈ B₁) ∧ (Y ω ∈ B₂)}`

where `B₁, B₂` are sets such that `B = B₁ ∩ B₂` and `B₁, B₂` are chosen such that `X ω ∈ B₁` and `Y ω ∈ B₂` implies `X ω ∩ Y ω ∈ B`.

A simple choice is to take `B₁ = B` and `B₂ = B`. Then we need:
`{ω | (X ω ∈ B) ∧ (Y ω ∈ B)}`

But we can express this as the intersection of `X⁻¹(B)` and `Y⁻¹(B)`:
`X⁻¹(B) ∩ Y⁻¹(B) = {ω | X ω ∈ B} ∩ {ω | Y ω ∈ B} = {ω | X ω ∈ B ∧ Y ω ∈ B}`

Since `X` and `Y` are `AEMeasurable`, `X⁻¹(B)` and `Y⁻¹(B)` are measurable. The intersection of two measurable sets is also measurable. Therefore, `X⁻¹(B) ∩ Y⁻¹(B)` is measurable, which implies `fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω` is `AEMeasurable`.

So, the proof should proceed by showing that the preimage of any measurable set under `fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω` is measurable. Let's write this out in Lean.

```tactics
import Mathlib.Probability.IdentDistrib
import Mathlib.Probability.ProductMeasure

/-!
# Bernoulli random variables

This file defines the binomial random distribution on a set. For a set `u : Set ι` and `p` between
`0` and `1`, this is the measure on `Set ι` such that each `i ∈ u` belongs to the random set with
probability `p`, and each `i ∉ u` doesn't belong to it.

## Notation

`Ber(u, p)` is the product of `p`-Bernoulli distributions on `u`.

## TODO

Add the characteristic predicate for a random variable to follow the Bernoulli distribution.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure unitInterval
open scoped Finset

namespace ProbabilityTheory
variable {ι Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {X Y : Ω → Set ι} {s u : Set ι} {i j : ι} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

variable (u p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on the set `u : Set V` is the measure on `Set V`
such that each element of `u` is taken with probability `p`, and the elements outside of `u` are
never taken. -/
noncomputable def bernoulliOn : Measure (Set ι) :=
  .comap (fun s i ↦ i ∈ s) <| infinitePi fun i ↦
    toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "Ber(" u ", " p ")" => bernoulliOn u p

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure Ber(u, p) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.isProbabilityMeasure_comap <| .of_forall fun P ↦
    ⟨{a | P a}, rfl⟩

variable (u p) in
lemma bernoulliOn_eq_map :
    Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i})
      (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) :=
  MeasurableEquiv.setOf.comap_symm

lemma bernoulliOn_apply (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun t a ↦ a ∈ t) '' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.measurableEmbedding.comap_apply ..

lemma bernoulliOn_apply' (S : Set (Set ι)) :
    Ber(u, p) S = (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
      ((fun p ↦ {a | p a}) ⁻¹' S) := by
  convert MeasurableEquiv.setOf.symm.comap_apply ..

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_zero : Ber(u, 0) = dirac ∅ := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

variable (u) in
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_one : Ber(u, 1) = dirac u := by simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]

section Countable
variable [Countable ι]

-- TODO: Does this hold if `ι` isn't countable? Note: the current proof only needs `u` cocountable,
-- but we don't bother doing this minigeneralisation.
lemma bernoulliOn_ae_subset : ∀ᵐ s ∂Ber(u, p), s ⊆ u := by
  classical
  change _ = _
  simp only [Set.compl_setOf, Set.not_subset_iff_exists_mem_notMem, Set.setOf_exists, Set.setOf_and,
    measure_iUnion_null_iff]
  rintro a
  by_cases ha : a ∈ u
  · simp [*]
  calc
    Ber(u, p) ({s | a ∈ s} ∩ {s | a ∉ u})
    _ = Ber(u, p) {s | a ∈ s} := by simp [ha]
    _ = infinitePi (fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False)
            (cylinder {a} {fun _ ↦ True}) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_apply']
      congr!
      ext
      simp [funext_iff]
    _ = 0 := by simp [infinitePi_cylinder _ (.singleton _), ha]

end Countable

variable (u p) in
-- TODO: Generalise to countable `ι` and finite `u`. See the TODO on `infinitePi_pi`.
@[simp] lemma bernoulliOn_singleton [Finite ι] (hsu : s ⊆ u) :
    Ber(u, p) {s} = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (u \ s).ncard := by
  classical
  cases nonempty_fintype ι
  lift u to Finset ι using Set.toFinite _
  calc
    Ber(u, p) {s}
    _ = ∏ i, ((if i ∈ u ↔ i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else 0) +
          if i ∈ s then 0 else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      simp [bernoulliOn_apply, Set.image_singleton, Set.indicator]
      simp [ENNReal.smul_def]
    _ = ∏ i ∈ u, (if i ∈ s then (toNNReal p : ENNReal) else (toNNReal (σ p) : ENNReal)) := by
      rw [← Finset.prod_subset u.subset_univ (by
        simpa +contextual [ite_add_ite, ← ENNReal.coe_add] using fun _ ↦ mt (@hsu _))]
      simp +contextual [ite_add_ite]
    _ = toNNReal p ^ s.ncard * toNNReal (σ p) ^ (↑u \ s).ncard := by
      simp [Finset.prod_ite, ← Set.ncard_coe_finset, Set.setOf_and,
        Set.inter_eq_right.2 hsu, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.diff_eq_compl_inter, Set.inter_comm]

/-! ### Bernoulli random variables -/

variable (X u p P) in
/-- A random variable `X : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBernoulliOn : Prop := HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P

lemma isBernoulliOn_congr (hXY : X =ᵐ[P] Y) : IsBernoulliOn X u p P ↔ IsBernoulliOn Y u p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hXY

lemma IsBernoulliOn.identDistrib_mem (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hi : i ∈ u) (hj : j ∈ u) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) (fun ω ↦ j ∈ X ω) P P where
  aemeasurable_fst := by fun_prop
  aemeasurable_snd := by fun_prop
  map_eq := sorry

lemma IsBernoulliOn.iIndepFun_mem (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun i ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) P := sorry

lemma IsBernoulliOn.sdiff (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ u \ X ω) u (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

lemma IsBernoulliOn.inter (hX : IsBernoulliOn X u p P) (hY : IsBernoulliOn Y u q P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ X ω ∩ Y ω) u (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```
